data.title: IQSTEL announces 51% acquisition of fintech GlobeTopper
data.description:
Telecommunication services provider IQSTEL (NASDAQ:IQST) announced on Thursday a 51% acquisition of the fintech GlobeTopper, with the transaction valued at up to $700K.

The definitive agreement comes as IQSTEL strives to become a $1B revenue corporation.

The transaction value includes $200K in staged cash payments and $500K in restricted IQSTEL common stock. Additionally, the acquisition includes performance-based earn-outs.

The transaction becomes effective July 1.

Craig Span is set to continue in his role as CEO of GlobeTopper.

data.title: Caleres suspends guidance amid tariff volatility and $15M SG&A cost reduction initiative
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Caleres (CAL) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Jay Schmidt, President and CEO, reported that "our first quarter results fell short of expectations. February sales were particularly weak, and although trends improved in March and in April, overall performance was below plan. Furthermore, operating earnings were pressured by lower gross margins, increased reserves and cost to cancel and move inventory." He noted that the operating environment had become more challenging, prompting the company to focus on optimizing sourcing strategy and reducing SG&A by $15 million annually through structural expense cuts.
  • Schmidt detailed impacts from the executive order on tariffs in April, stating, "we acted quickly to pause our production in China... each of these decisions had associated costs that impacted the quarter." He also highlighted brand performance, with Sam Edelman showing domestic and double-digit international sales growth and Famous Footwear launching Jordan in 147 stores as an exclusive in the Shoe Chain channel.
  • "For the first quarter, sales were $614.2 million, down 6.8%. This was below our expectations for sales, down 5% to 6%," stated CFO Jack Calandra. He added, "consolidated gross margin was 45.4%, down 150 basis points to last year and was driven by lower margins in both segments."

Outlook

  • Calandra announced, "given the continued uncertainty, we are suspending guidance at this time." He provided that Famous Footwear's quarter-to-date comparable sales were "slightly better than 1Q's, down 4.6%." Direct-to-consumer sales in the brand portfolio increased modestly year over year. Management expects the $15 million SG&A reduction to begin in Q3, with $7.5 million savings realized in the back half of 2025.
  • Management described the sourcing environment as "fluid" and intends to remain "nimble," with only 10% of sourcing dollars expected from China in the back half of the year.

Financial Results

  • Caleres reported Q1 adjusted earnings per share of $0.22 and sales of $614.2 million. Brand portfolio sales declined 6.9%. Tariff-related challenges and discrete items reduced sales by about $7 million compared to last year. Famous Footwear sales declined 6.3% and comparable sales were down 4.6%. Consolidated gross margin fell to 45.4%, driven by lower segment margins and increased reserves. Operating earnings were $12.2 million and operating margin was 2%.
  • Inventory ended at $573.6 million, up $43 million or 8.1% from last year, with the increase coming from both Famous and the brand portfolio. Cash flow from operations was negative $5.7 million, attributed to lower net income and higher inventory levels.

Q&A

  • Dana Telsey, Telsey Advisory Group: Asked about price increases, gross margin outlook, potential restructuring or brand exits, back-to-school inventory, and SG&A cuts. Schmidt stated that price increases are "brand by brand" and will begin with fall receipts. He said, "we don't have anything to announce" on restructuring but will continue to review. On back-to-school, "so far, we're in a pretty good position" and Famous will have 53 FLAIR stores for the season. Calandra clarified that "these reductions are permanent and structural in nature" and $7.5 million savings will be realized evenly in Q3 and Q4.
  • Laura Champine, Loop Capital Markets: Questioned the impact of the Jordan launch and tariff changes. Calandra responded that the Jordan launch was "very new" with "fairly limited marketing at this point" but the company is "very excited about the potential." Schmidt emphasized no immediate change in China sourcing despite tariff news, describing the environment as "fluid."
  • Mitch Kummetz, Seaport Research: Sought details on the Jordan launch's impact and cost pressures. Schmidt did not provide specific lift data but highlighted strong early sell-through, especially in men's and boys. On tariffs, Schmidt said, "it's very new and I think we're still... trying to get as many answers to it right now." Calandra broke down cost impacts: inventory markdown reserves for spring 2025 are believed to be well-covered, while tariff-related costs remain "TBD."
  • Chris Broszell (for Ashley Owens), KeyBanc: Asked about the persistence of operating earnings pressures. Schmidt indicated that inventory reserves are not expected to be a continuing headwind, while tariff-related impacts are unpredictable. Price increases are expected to be a smaller contributor to cost offset.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concern about inventory levels, the impact of tariffs, and margin pressure, with a generally cautious tone. Telsey's multi-part questions and Champine's focus on trend shifts and tariffs reflected skepticism about near-term recovery.
  • Management maintained a defensive stance, repeatedly describing the environment as "fluid" and emphasizing efforts to optimize costs and sourcing. Schmidt and Calandra used hedging language such as "we believe," "we are confident," and "we will remain nimble."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst tone shifted from cautious optimism about new initiatives to deeper concern about operational challenges. Management's tone became more defensive and focused on immediate mitigation, a change from the prior quarter's emphasis on strategic investments and growth.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The most significant change was the suspension of company guidance amid tariff uncertainty, compared to the previous quarter's issuance of full-year and Q1 guidance. In Q4 2024, management expressed confidence in sequential improvement and detailed multiple growth drivers, while in Q1 2025, the focus shifted to cost containment and risk management.
  • Key metrics deteriorated: Q4 adjusted EPS was $0.33, while Q1 2025 was $0.22; Q4 sales were $639.2 million vs. Q1's $614.2 million. Inventory increased from $565 million to $573.6 million.
  • Analysts in the previous quarter probed for upside in contemporary and international brands; in the current quarter, their focus shifted to risk, inventory, tariffs, and cost structure.
  • Management's confidence in growth initiatives, such as FLAIR stores and brand launches, was tempered by the suspension of guidance and emphasis on cost controls.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited challenges from tariff escalation, sourcing disruptions, increased inventory levels, and worsening customer credit issues. They noted the impact of tariffs as "fluid" and unpredictable and are monitoring the situation for further actions.
  • Calandra explained that strategies to offset costs include "factory concessions, lower gross margin and select price increases." Inventory risks remain, but management believes reserves are sufficient for spring 2025 product.
  • Analysts raised concerns about margin sustainability, brand performance variability, and the timing and effectiveness of cost reductions.

Final Takeaway

Caleres management acknowledged a challenging quarter with underperformance in sales and margins, driven by tariff-related disruptions, elevated inventories, and customer credit concerns. The company has suspended forward guidance due to ongoing volatility but is executing a $15 million annualized SG&A reduction to offset pressures. While management remains optimistic about long-term opportunities, including the integration of Stuart Weitzman and growth from FLAIR stores and brand launches, the near-term focus is on cost control, sourcing agility, and navigating a fluid external environment.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Build-A-Bear updates 2025 pre-tax guidance to $61M-$67M while expanding global footprint
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Build-A-Bear Workshop, Inc. (BBW) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Sharon Price John, President and CEO, highlighted, "we have reported the best first quarter results in Build-A-Bear’s history. Double-digit top-line growth drove record revenue, margin expansion, and strong performance across all segments. This represents impressive earnings per share growth of nearly 43%." She cited the company’s ongoing strategy of expanding new product categories, reaching the over-18 kidult segment, and growing international presence, now in 30% of countries. John stated, "Revenue increased 11.9% to more than $128 million. Pre-tax income grew 30.6% to nearly $20 million, and EPS increased 42.7% to $1.17. Additionally, we returned over $7 million in capital to shareholders." John reiterated 2025 revenue guidance, with an update to pre-tax guidance due to tariffs.
  • Christopher Hurt, Chief Operations and Experience Officer, described global footprint expansion: "We opened 15 net new experience locations in the first quarter, highlighted by the opening of our corporately-operated Dublin, Ireland location... As we continue to grow the brand in more places, we’ve added two more countries, bringing our total count to 30%." Hurt confirmed expansion plans remain on track, expecting at least 50 net new locations for 2025.
  • Voin Todorovic, Chief Financial Officer, stated, "This was the most profitable first quarter in the company’s history. We grew across all segments, expanded the gross profit margin, and increased pre-tax income to a new record. This reflects the work from our strategic initiatives over the past several years and the business operating at a higher level of profitability."

Outlook

  • The company maintained its 2025 revenue guidance and continues to expect the addition of at least 50 net new experience locations, mostly operated by international partners. Commercial segment revenue is expected to grow at least 20% for the year. The company updated pre-tax income guidance to a range of $61 million to $67 million, inclusive of current tariff rates. Todorovic said, "Given Build-A-Bear’s solid business fundamentals and assuming tariffs remain at current levels, we expect to report record revenue for the year and maintain strong profitability with double-digit pre-tax margins."

Financial Results

  • Build-A-Bear reported total revenues of $128.4 million for the quarter, an increase of 11.9%. Net retail sales were $119.6 million, up 10.9%. All four store levers—traffic, conversion, average unit retail, and units per transaction—were positive. Domestic store traffic rose 3%, while national U.S. traffic declined by 3%. E-commerce demand increased 0.5%. Commercial revenue, including international franchise revenue, grew 28.3%. Gross margin reached 56.8%, a 260 basis point improvement. SG&A expenses were $53.7 million or 41.7% of revenues. Pre-tax income reached $19.6 million, representing 15.3% of revenues. EPS was $1.17. Cash balance at quarter end was $44.3 million, after returning $37 million to shareholders over 12 months. Inventory was $72.3 million, attributed to accelerated core product purchases and nearly $2 million in tariff costs.

Q&A

  • Eric Beder, SCC Research: Asked about leveraging movie tie-ins and flexibility to respond to pop culture trends. Sharon Price John explained the company maintains strong ties with major film creators like Disney and is prepared to capitalize on such events, but now views them as incremental rather than core to the business. She also detailed improvements in inventory management systems, enabling faster, more accurate responses to social media trends, saying, "We’re much more flexible now... will increase our confidence in even going longer on some of these things that we think are going to be hot."
  • Keegan Cox, D.A. Davidson: Asked about retail segment performance and partner-operated store strategy. Voin Todorovic responded, "All four levers were positive. Traffic was about 3%... higher traffic, we were able to increase our conversion, and... drive units per transaction, as well as with lower discounts, our average unit retail has gone up." Sharon Price John and Christopher Hurt described the high standards and careful selection process for international partners.
  • Greg Gibas, Northland Securities: Inquired about Mini Beans expansion and updated outlook for tariffs. Sharon Price John said Mini Beans have been introduced in retailers outside workshops and domestically in select locations. Christopher Hurt added they are now in 50 Applegreen stores and several Hudson airport locations. Voin Todorovic clarified, "our new pre-tax range, now $61 million to $67 million. This range reflects less than $10 million net of mitigation impact of tariffs and associated costs."
  • Steve Silver, Argus Research: Asked about international expansion and in-store traffic trends. Sharon Price John described the value of working with local market experts and the enthusiasm demonstrated by international partners. On Q1 traffic, she stated, "between 60% and 80% of trips that are made to Build-A-Bear are planned in advance," reinforcing Build-A-Bear's status as a destination retailer.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for details on tariff mitigation, partner strategy, product expansion, and inventory flexibility, reflecting a slightly positive to neutral tone as they sought insights on growth and risk management.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone throughout, especially in prepared remarks. Sharon Price John said, "We believe we are well positioned to manage an environment where uncertainty has become the norm rather than the exception." In Q&A, management responses were detailed and reinforced confidence in their strategy.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone remains confident but slightly more focused on cost pressures from tariffs. Analyst questions in both quarters probed for risk management and growth levers.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue growth accelerated to 11.9% in Q1 2025 from a full-year increase of 3.6% in FY 2024. Gross margin improved from 56.6% in Q4 to 56.8% in Q1. Q1 pre-tax income margin was 15.3%, compared to 18.3% for Q4. Management reiterated guidance for at least 50 net new experience locations in 2025 and updated pre-tax income guidance to $61 million-$67 million, versus previous commentary noting caution due to tariffs. The tone continued to emphasize resilience and strategy execution, but with greater detail on tariff mitigation. Analysts continued to focus on store performance, product expansion, and risk management, with similar sentiment as prior quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing tariff costs, with an expected impact of less than $10 million for 2025 after mitigation efforts. Additional headwinds include $5 million in medical and labor costs. Todorovic outlined sourcing diversification, reduced reliance on China, and the ability to redirect China-sourced products to international locations as key mitigation strategies. Inventory was increased to manage tariff risk. Analysts raised questions around the sustainability of growth, partner quality, and flexibility in inventory management.

Final Takeaway

Build-A-Bear Workshop delivered its strongest first quarter ever, driven by double-digit revenue growth, robust retail and commercial segment performance, and ongoing global expansion. Management reaffirmed revenue guidance for 2025 and updated pre-tax income expectations in response to tariffs, while highlighting successful product innovation and diversification strategies. With a record-setting start to the year and solid momentum into the second quarter, the company remains focused on executing its strategic initiatives, expanding its international presence, and maintaining strong profitability in a dynamic environment.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: ATS projects fiscal Q1 2026 revenue in $680M–$730M range amid record $2.1B backlog and EV settlement
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: ATS Corporation (ATS) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Andrew Hider, Chief Executive Officer, highlighted the negotiated settlement with the company's EV customer, stating, "It was important that we put this matter behind us to enable us to continue to execute on our growth strategy." He reported that Q4 was "the third highest bookings quarter in the company history and included the organic growth along with contributions from acquisitions." Hider noted that life sciences businesses showed "strength and market leadership," with further expansion from the Paxiom and Heidolph acquisitions.
  • Hider further stated, "Order bookings for the quarter were $863 million, up 9% from the fourth quarter last year," and confirmed a "record" $3.3 billion in full year bookings, positioning ATS well for fiscal '26.
  • Hider explained that the order backlog ended the quarter at approximately $2.1 billion, the highest in the last eight quarters, and emphasized the company's "well-diversified" opportunity funnel and confidence in driving growth and shareholder value in fiscal '26.
  • Ryan McLeod, Chief Financial Officer, addressed the EV settlement: "We expect to receive USD 134.75 million or about CAD 194 million...before the end of fiscal Q1. This settlement resulted in an after-tax impact of approximately CAD 129 million, which was reflected in our Q4 results." McLeod clarified the settlement's impact on financial reporting and stated, "Importantly, the settlement will reduce our net debt to adjusted EBITDA leverage by 0.5x or approximately half a turn, providing us with greater flexibility to continue to execute on our growth strategy."

Outlook

  • McLeod provided specific guidance: "We ended the fiscal year with an order backlog of approximately $2.1 billion, and we expect Q1 revenues to be in the range of $680 million to $730 million."
  • Hider stated, "Given our Q4 order bookings, we remain optimistic. ATS is well positioned in regulated markets with strategic customer relationships." The company continues to monitor macroeconomic factors and is focused on mitigating short-term disruptions through supply chain optimization and targeted price action.
  • McLeod indicated that expanding operating margins is a priority, with expectations for "improvement throughout fiscal '26" as cost structure changes and volume alignment in EV businesses take effect.

Financial Results

  • McLeod reported, "Order bookings were $863 million, an increase of 9% over Q4 last year and included 2.6% organic growth, a 4% contribution from acquisitions and a 2.5% positive impact from foreign exchange translation."
  • After adjusting for the revenue portion of the EV settlement, Q4 revenues were $721 million, down 8.9% compared to last year, with sequential revenue growth of 10.6% from Q3.
  • Fourth quarter adjusted earnings from operations were $74.3 million, a 23% decline from the prior year, attributed to lower revenue volumes, especially in transportation. Gross margin for Q4 was 29%, a 90 basis point improvement from last year.
  • Adjusted EPS for the quarter was $0.41. Cash flows from operating activities were $39.3 million, with capital expenditures and intangible asset investments totaling $29 million for Q4.

Q&A

  • Cherilyn Radbourne, TD Cowen, asked about confidence in returning to positive organic growth in fiscal '26. Hider responded, "Short answer is very positive...we are confident we'll be returning to growth," referencing the "trailing 12-month book-to-bill ratio, 1.23 really supports that alignment."
  • Radbourne questioned the internal control deficiency. McLeod explained it was identified through "our normal course process" following the adoption of SOX requirements and clarified, "none of this had a...impact on our financial...statements." The company is focused on documentation improvements.
  • Maxim Sytchev, National Bank, asked about the sustainability of GLP-1 growth in healthcare backlog. Hider stated ATS is "working with 8-plus customers" in GLP-1 and is also active in radiopharmaceuticals and wearable devices, describing the outlook as "cautiously optimistic."
  • Sytchev also asked about working capital improvements. McLeod confirmed working capital is "just over the 15% target" excluding EV settlement, with efficiency initiatives underway.
  • Sabahat Khan, RBC Capital Markets, asked about backlog conversion rates and margin progression. McLeod explained conversion rates will "change...driven just by material flows and different factors," and expects margin expansion to be "modest" but progressing through fiscal '26.
  • David Ocampo, Cormark Securities, inquired about redeployment of EV-related inventory and contract assets. McLeod said contract assets are zero and inventory is written down to expected utilization, with no material benefit expected from redeployment.
  • Michael Glen, Raymond James, asked about U.S. product manufacturing and CapEx. McLeod indicated there are no significant moves planned at this time.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism regarding growth prospects, but raised questions about backlog conversion, working capital, and exposure to macroeconomic risks. The tone was neutral, with some pressing for clarity on margin expansion and capital allocation.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone throughout, using phrases such as "we are confident," and "our view of the year is cautiously optimistic." During Q&A, management addressed concerns directly and provided detailed explanations without defensive language.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst sentiment was slightly more constructive, as analysts noted improvements in booking trends and backlog. Management's tone remained consistent, emphasizing resilience and strategic execution.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language became more specific, with Q1 revenue guidance provided for fiscal '26. Strategic focus shifted to leveraging the EV settlement to improve balance sheet flexibility and accelerate growth initiatives.
  • Key metrics showed a sequential improvement in revenues and gross margin, while adjusted earnings and EPS declined year-over-year. The order backlog remained stable at $2.1 billion, but order bookings decreased from $883 million in Q3 to $863 million in Q4.
  • Analysts in both quarters focused on backlog conversion, margin expansion, and capital allocation, but Q4 questions reflected greater attention to the impact and deployment of the EV settlement.
  • Management's confidence in returning to organic growth in fiscal '26 was more pronounced in Q4, with increased emphasis on diversified markets and operational efficiency.

Risks and Concerns

  • Macroeconomic uncertainty, geopolitical and trade tensions, and potential impacts from tariffs were highlighted. Hider cautioned, "we have not seen any material change in customer behavior to date," but acknowledged possible impacts if uncertainty continues.
  • Management is mitigating risks by optimizing supply chain, strengthening regional capabilities, and using localized supply strategies. Contract structures are designed to pass tariff costs to customers when possible.
  • Analyst concerns centered on backlog conversion, working capital intensity, and exposure to industry cycles, particularly in transportation and life sciences.

Final Takeaway

ATS Corporation enters fiscal 2026 supported by a record order backlog, strong bookings, and a more flexible balance sheet following resolution of the EV customer dispute. Management is confident in returning to organic growth, with a well-diversified market exposure and ongoing efficiency initiatives aimed at margin expansion. While macroeconomic and trade uncertainties persist, the company is actively managing risks and remains focused on delivering value to customers and shareholders through innovation, operational discipline, and strategic capital deployment.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Meta partners with Anduril for XR Army headsets
data.description:
Meta (NASDAQ:META) is partnering with Palmer Luckey’s Anduril Industries to develop and build a range of extended reality (“XR”) gear for the U.S. Army to enhance soldiers’ sight and hearing, along with capabilities that allow for advanced interaction with artificial intelligence weapons systems.

By capitalizing on Meta’s (NASDAQ:META) virtual reality and augmented reality (“VR/AR”) technology with Anduril’s AI-powered command and control Lattice platform, EagleEye will provide soldiers with enhanced perception and intuitive control of autonomous platforms on the battlefield.

Although the two companies have already submitted a bid to the Pentagon for the $100M AR/VR hardware contract, the development of the headsets has already been funded through private capital with the intention of finding other buyers within the U.S. military. The contract is part of a larger $22B project by the U.S. Army to develop wearable combat technology.

“I am glad to be working with Meta once again.” said Anduril founder Palmer Luckey. “My mission has long been to turn warfighters into technomancers, and the products we are building with Meta do just that.”

The partnership to support U.S. soldiers isn't the first collaboration between Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg and Palmer Luckey. Their working relationship dates back to 2014, when Meta (then Facebook) acquired Luckey’s Oculus VR startup for $2 billion. Luckey joined the company to continue developing the technology but was dismissed two years later, reportedly due to his political affiliations.

Luckey went on to start Anduril with a mission of “transforming defense capabilities with advanced technology” that advances the military’s autonomous defense systems through its Lattice AI system.  

And for Meta (NASDAQ:META) embrace of the U.S. military is part of a more strategic goal to capture multi-million-dollar Pentagon contracts under a new administration. The company has recently opened up its Llama AI model for military use and advertised open jobs for public policy managers to “lead our outreach to national security and foreign policy agencies within the Executive Branch.”

“If you’re looking for a money spigot that’s never going to get turned off, you go to the Pentagon,” summed up George Washington Associate Director for International Affairs Shana Marshall to Forbes.

data.title: Amazon forming new group to focus on 'breakthrough' consumer products: report
data.description:
• Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) has formed a new group inside the technology giant to focus on “breakthrough” consumer products, CNBC reported.
  • Amazon did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • The group, known as ZeroOne, will be led by former Microsoft (MSFT) executive J. Allard, the news outlet added. Allard is credited with having helped create the Xbox gaming console and its Zune music player. According to Allard's LinkedIn page, he became vice president of ZeroOne in September 2024.
  • The ZeroOne team will be based in Seattle (where Amazon is headquartered), San Francisco, and Sunnyvale, Calif., the news outlet added, citing recent job postings. 
  • Amazon has had mixed success with its consumer technology products, which include its Kindle tablets and e-readers, Alexa lineup of speakers and Fire TV streaming products. The company also launched a Fire-branded smartphone in 2014, but poor sales and reception from consumers led to it being discontinued in 2015.
  • In February, Amazon held a splashy event in New York where it showed off a new artificial intelligence-powered version of its Alexa voice assistant.
  • Amazon shares rose 1.1% in midday trading on Thursday.

data.title: Disney adds more subscriber perks to its Disney+ and Hulu services
data.description:
Walt Disney Company (NYSE:DIS) is expanding the perks available for subscribers to its flagship Disney+ streaming service. Subscribers who sign up for the Disney+ program through the Disney+ website will have access to perks such as discounts on Disney (NYSE:DIS) resorts, free items in video games, and a $10 credit at Disney Pinnacle by Dapper Labs, which was noted to sell collectible pins. Subscribers will also be able to enter contests to win a Disney (DIS) cruise and attend movie premieres.

At the end of Q1, Disney+ had approximately 124.6 million subscribers worldwide, which was a slight decrease from the 125.3 million reported at the end of Q4. Disney+ peaked with 153.8 million subscribers at the end of Q3 of 2024. Currently, about 30% of current Disney+ subscribers are on the ad-supported tier

Disney (DIS) also unveiled a perks program for Hulu subscribers. Starting June 2, the program will give subscribers a chance to win tickets to Jimmy Kimmel Live! , Comic-Con and the Lollapalooza music festival. Customers who subscribe to both Disney+ and Hulu through various packages will be able to access the perks programs from both.

"Our fans are some of the most passionate in the world, and perks are our way of thanking them for subscribing to Disney+," stated Disney (DIS) Executive Vice President Samantha Rosenberg about the new features. 

Shares of Disney (DIS) edged 0.2% lower in Thursday afternoon trading.

data.title: Fed Chair Jerome Powell meets with President Trump to discuss economy
data.description:
Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell met with President Donald Trump at the president's invitation on Thursday "to discuss economic developments, including for growth, employment, and inflation," the central bank said in a statement.

Powell didn't discuss his expectations for monetary policy, other than to "stress that the path of policy will depend entirely on incoming economic information and what that means for the outlook," the statement said.

He also said that he and his colleagues on the Federal Open Market Committee, will "set monetary policy, as required by law, to support maximum employment and stable prices." Those decisions will be "based solely on careful, objective, and non-political analysis," the Fed said.

After the last FOMC meeting, which took place May 7, Trump called Powell a "fool" for not cutting interest rates. In April, he had said Powell's termination "cannot come fast enough," but later said he had no intention of firing the Fed chair.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Nutrien plans new Pacific Northwest terminal to increase potash exports
data.description:
Nutrien (NYSE:NTR) is planning to build a major terminal at a Pacific Northwest port and is exploring sites in the U.S. and Canada, CEO Ken Seitz told The Globe and Mail in an interview Wednesday.

The company is searching for a deepwater port with rail infrastructure that can handle bulk potash exports for fast-growing markets in the Indo-Pacific, including China, India and Japan, Seitz said, while refusing to comment on which ports are in the running.

Regulations, taxes and approval timelines will influence Nutrien's (NYSE:NTR) decision about whether to choose the U.S. or Canada, the CEO said, adding that the site should be selected by the end of 2025 and be operational by the end of the decade.

The expansion is part of Nutrien's (NTR) long-term strategy to capture market share in countries with maturing agricultural industries where fertilizer demand is forecast to rise; global demand for potash currently totals 71M-75M metric tons, which the company forecasts to grow to 80M-85M tons by the end of the decade.

However, building major infrastructure in Canada to meet this demand is "very challenging" due to drawn-out regulatory timelines, Seitz said.

Prime Minister Carney's federal government has promised to slash timelines, and potash is included on the list of 34 Canadian critical minerals - good news, Seitz said, that may factor into the company's decision making on the new terminal.

data.title: CME Group, CF Benchmarks to launch crypto reference rates, indices
data.description:
CME Group (NASDAQ:CME) and the crypto benchmark indices provider CF Benchmarks announced on Thursday plans to launch four new cryptocurrency reference rates and real-time indices for Arbitrum, Ondo (ONDO-USD), Near (NEAR-USD), and Sui (SUI-USD).

The reference rates and indices are not tradable futures products.
